name: Lord Buckley
abstract:
Lord Richard Buckley (born Richard Myrle Buckley; April 5, 1906 – November 12, 1960) was an American stand-up comedian and recording artist, who in the 1940s and 1950s created a character that was, according to The New York Times, "an unlikely persona ... part English royalty, part Dizzy Gillespie."
Michael Packenham, writing in The Balt…

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: And Lord Buckley -- Dick Buckley. They had shows, but they would let us in to come do a spot, and people would throw out coins, money on the floor.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: Well I’m a fan of Lester Young and his lingo, and Lenny Bruce, and Lord Buckley, and all those hipsters.
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, November 11, 2017 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: Lord Buckley said, “to err is human and to forgive is a gasser.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 29, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]
